intent: Current rules.d uses %exes=/bin/a then allow perm=open exe=%exes : all; snapshot was taken when the rule used the literal form exe=/bin/a (no set definition). Canonical predicate is identical (exe=%exes expands and sorts to the same value as exe=/bin/a per f2 section 4.1). Expected drift: EMPTY. RED against current impl because row_key uses source-order render ('exe=%exes' vs 'exe=/bin/a' are different strings).

oracle.method: f2 section 4.1 canonical predicate equality: a %set reference and its sorted expanded members are the same grant; no drift must be produced
oracle.grounding: canonical_grant_key in register.rs expands SetRefs via canonical_value; row_key must use canonical_grant_key not source-order render
